coreCompetencies: Basic grasp of robot components, sensors and actuators
Elementary command of forward kinematics and coordinate frames
Ability to program simple robot behaviors under supervision
Basic practice reading sensor data such as encoders and range finders
Ability to run basic control loops for motors and joints
Elementary command of the Robot Operating System with its nodes and topics
Basic grasp of robot simulation environments
Ability to document robotic setups and wiring
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with microcontroller platforms for prototyping
Familiarity with basic simulators such as Gazebo
Elementary sense of gripper and end-effector control

coreCompetencies: Ability to implement feedback control such as PID controllers
Ability to build basic path planning for mobile robots
Practical command of rigid-body dynamics and forces
Ability to fuse data from multiple sensors for state estimation
Grasp of localization methods for mobile robots
Ability to perform basic manipulation and pick-and-place tasks
Practice with robot perception using cameras and depth sensors
Ability to work within ROS packages and message interfaces
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with mobile robot navigation stacks
Sense of industrial robot programming
Familiarity with coordinate transforms and calibration

coreCompetencies: Command of designing complete robotic systems from perception to actuation
Ability to implement simultaneous localization and mapping
Ability to build motion planning with obstacle avoidance
Solid command of control theory including state-space methods
Ability to implement autonomous navigation in real environments
Ability to design human-robot interaction for shared workspaces
Practice coordinating multiple robots on a task
Ability to apply learning-based methods to robot control
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with transferring policies from simulation to real robots
Ability to work with manipulators and inverse kinematics
Sense of drone or legged robot autonomy
indicators: Leads robotics projects with minimal supervision

coreCompetencies: Ability to architect robotic platforms integrating hardware and software
Command of perception pipelines fusing SLAM, vision and depth
Ability to design whole-body and coordinated multi-joint control
Solid command of motion and task planning under constraints
Ability to bridge the simulation-to-reality gap in deployment
Ability to apply learning from demonstration to robot skills
Practice with functional safety requirements for robots around people
Ability to mentor engineers on robotics design and integration
complementaryCompetencies: Experience with manipulation in unstructured environments
Familiarity with real-time systems for robot control
Sense of certification for safety-critical robots
indicators: Makes architectural decisions on robot systems

coreCompetencies: Expertise in leading robotics programs from prototype to product
Ability to design and operate fleets of coordinated robots

Ability to define automation strategy for a line of robotic products

Familiarity with teleoperation and shared autonomy
Management of hardware and component supplier relationships
indicators: Designs robotics architectures for complete products

coreCompetencies: Ability to architect robotics platforms serving multiple product lines
Expertise in safety certification and compliance for deployed robots

Ability to design autonomous systems that meet safety targets
Solid command of the risks specific to robots operating among people

Expertise in managing the total cost of robot fleet operations
Ability to set standards for perception, control and safety
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with emerging robot hardware and actuation
Ability to design shared perception and navigation services
Sense of edge computing for on-robot processing
indicators: Architects robotics solutions for multiple teams
Represents the company technically in robotics
